speaker: KENT
text: I thought the King had more affected the Duke

speaker: KENT
text: of Albany than Cornwall.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: It did always seem so to us, but now in

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: the division of the kingdom, it appears not which

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: of the dukes he values most, for equalities are so

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: weighed that curiosity in neither can make choice

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: of either’s moiety.

speaker: KENT
text: Is not this your son, my lord?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: His breeding, sir, hath been at my

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: charge. I have so often blushed to acknowledge

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: him that now I am brazed to ’t.

speaker: KENT
text: I cannot conceive you.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sir, this young fellow’s mother could,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: whereupon she grew round-wombed and had indeed,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: sir, a son for her cradle ere she had a husband

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: for her bed. Do you smell a fault?

speaker: KENT
text: I cannot wish the fault undone, the issue of it

speaker: KENT
text: being so proper.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But I have a son, sir, by order of law,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: some year elder than this, who yet is no dearer in

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: my account. Though this knave came something

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: saucily to the world before he was sent for, yet was

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: his mother fair, there was good sport at his making,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: and the whoreson must be acknowledged.—Do you

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: know this noble gentleman, Edmund?

speaker: EDMUND
text: No, my lord.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: My lord of Kent. Remember him hereafter

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: as my honorable friend.

speaker: EDMUND
text: My services to your Lordship.

speaker: KENT
text: I must love you and sue to know you better.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Sir, I shall study deserving.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: He hath been out nine years, and away he

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text:
shall again.
                 
                (
                Sennet
                .
                )
               The King is coming.

speaker: LEAR
text: Attend the lords of France and Burgundy, Gloucester.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I shall, my lord.

speaker: LEAR
text: Meantime we shall express our darker purpose.—

speaker: LEAR
text: Give me the map there.

speaker: LEAR
text: Know that we have divided

speaker: LEAR
text: In three our kingdom, and ’tis our fast intent

speaker: LEAR
text: To shake all cares and business from our age,

speaker: LEAR
text: Conferring them on younger strengths, while we

speaker: LEAR
text: Unburdened crawl toward death. Our son of Cornwall

speaker: LEAR
text: And you, our no less loving son of Albany,

speaker: LEAR
text: We have this hour a constant will to publish

speaker: LEAR
text: Our daughters’ several dowers, that future strife

speaker: LEAR
text: May be prevented now.

speaker: LEAR
text: The two great princes, France and Burgundy,

speaker: LEAR
text: Great rivals in our youngest daughter’s love,

speaker: LEAR
text: Long in our court have made their amorous sojourn

speaker: LEAR
text: And here are to be answered. Tell me, my daughters—

speaker: LEAR
text: Since now we will divest us both of rule,

speaker: LEAR
text: Interest of territory, cares of state—

speaker: LEAR
text: Which of you shall we say doth love us most,

speaker: LEAR
text: That we our largest bounty may extend

speaker: LEAR
text: Where nature doth with merit challenge. Goneril,

speaker: LEAR
text: Our eldest born, speak first.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Sir, I love you more than word can wield the matter,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Dearer than eyesight, space, and liberty,

speaker: GONERIL
text: Beyond what can be valued, rich or rare,

speaker: GONERIL
text: No less than life, with grace, health, beauty, honor;

speaker: GONERIL
text: As much as child e’er loved, or father found;

speaker: GONERIL
text: A love that makes breath poor, and speech unable.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Beyond all manner of so much I love you.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: What shall Cordelia speak? Love, and be silent.

speaker: LEAR
text: Of all these bounds, even from this line to this,

speaker: LEAR
text: With shadowy forests and with champains riched,

speaker: LEAR
text: With plenteous rivers and wide-skirted meads,

speaker: LEAR
text: We make thee lady. To thine and Albany’s issue

speaker: LEAR
text: Be this perpetual.—What says our second daughter,

speaker: LEAR
text: Our dearest Regan, wife of Cornwall? Speak.

speaker: REGAN
text: I am made of that self mettle as my sister

speaker: REGAN
text: And prize me at her worth. In my true heart

speaker: REGAN
text: I find she names my very deed of love;

speaker: REGAN
text: Only she comes too short, that I profess

speaker: REGAN
text: Myself an enemy to all other joys

speaker: REGAN
text: Which the most precious square of sense possesses,

speaker: REGAN
text: And find I am alone felicitate

speaker: REGAN
text: In your dear Highness’ love.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Then poor Cordelia!

speaker: CORDELIA
text: And yet not so, since I am sure my love’s

speaker: CORDELIA
text: More ponderous than my tongue.

speaker: LEAR
text: To thee and thine hereditary ever

speaker: LEAR
text: Remain this ample third of our fair kingdom,

speaker: LEAR
text: No less in space, validity, and pleasure

speaker: LEAR
text: Than that conferred on Goneril.—Now, our joy,

speaker: LEAR
text: Although our last and least, to whose young love

speaker: LEAR
text: The vines of France and milk of Burgundy

speaker: LEAR
text: Strive to be interessed, what can you say to draw

speaker: LEAR
text: A third more opulent than your sisters’? Speak.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Nothing, my lord.

speaker: LEAR
text: Nothing?

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Nothing.

speaker: LEAR
text: Nothing will come of nothing. Speak again.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Unhappy that I am, I cannot heave

speaker: CORDELIA
text: My heart into my mouth. I love your Majesty

speaker: CORDELIA
text: According to my bond, no more nor less.

speaker: LEAR
text: How, how, Cordelia? Mend your speech a little,

speaker: LEAR
text: Lest you may mar your fortunes.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Good my lord,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: You have begot me, bred me, loved me.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: I return those duties back as are right fit:

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Obey you, love you, and most honor you.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Why have my sisters husbands if they say

speaker: CORDELIA
text: They love you all? Haply, when I shall wed,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: That lord whose hand must take my plight shall carry

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Half my love with him, half my care and duty.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Sure I shall never marry like my sisters,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: To love my father all.

speaker: LEAR
text: But goes thy heart with this?

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Ay, my good lord.

speaker: LEAR
text: So young and so untender?

speaker: CORDELIA
text: So young, my lord, and true.

speaker: LEAR
text: Let it be so. Thy truth, then, be thy dower,

speaker: LEAR
text: For by the sacred radiance of the sun,

speaker: LEAR
text: The mysteries of Hecate and the night,

speaker: LEAR
text: By all the operation of the orbs

speaker: LEAR
text: From whom we do exist and cease to be,

speaker: LEAR
text: Here I disclaim all my paternal care,

speaker: LEAR
text: Propinquity, and property of blood,

speaker: LEAR
text: And as a stranger to my heart and me

speaker: LEAR
text: Hold thee from this forever. The barbarous Scythian,

speaker: LEAR
text: Or he that makes his generation messes

speaker: LEAR
text: To gorge his appetite, shall to my bosom

speaker: LEAR
text: Be as well neighbored, pitied, and relieved

speaker: LEAR
text: As thou my sometime daughter.

speaker: KENT
text: Good my liege—

speaker: LEAR
text: Peace, Kent.

speaker: LEAR
text: Come not between the dragon and his wrath.

speaker: LEAR
text: I loved her most and thought to set my rest

speaker: LEAR
text:
On her kind nursery.
                 
                To
                 
                Cordelia
                .
               Hence and avoid my sight!—

speaker: LEAR
text: So be my grave my peace as here I give

speaker: LEAR
text: Her father’s heart from her.—Call France. Who stirs?

speaker: LEAR
text:
Call Burgundy.
                 
                An
                 
                Attendant
                 
                exits
                .
               Cornwall and Albany,

speaker: LEAR
text: With my two daughters’ dowers digest the third.

speaker: LEAR
text: Let pride, which she calls plainness, marry her.

speaker: LEAR
text: I do invest you jointly with my power,

speaker: LEAR
text: Preeminence, and all the large effects

speaker: LEAR
text: That troop with majesty. Ourself by monthly course,

speaker: LEAR
text: With reservation of an hundred knights

speaker: LEAR
text: By you to be sustained, shall our abode

speaker: LEAR
text: Make with you by due turn. Only we shall retain

speaker: LEAR
text: The name and all th’ addition to a king.

speaker: LEAR
text: The sway, revenue, execution of the rest,

speaker: LEAR
text: Belovèd sons, be yours, which to confirm,

speaker: LEAR
text: This coronet part between you.

speaker: KENT
text: Royal Lear,

speaker: KENT
text: Whom I have ever honored as my king,

speaker: KENT
text: Loved as my father, as my master followed,

speaker: KENT
text: As my great patron thought on in my prayers—

speaker: LEAR
text: The bow is bent and drawn. Make from the shaft.

speaker: KENT
text: Let it fall rather, though the fork invade

speaker: KENT
text: The region of my heart. Be Kent unmannerly

speaker: KENT
text: When Lear is mad. What wouldst thou do, old man?

speaker: KENT
text: Think’st thou that duty shall have dread to speak

speaker: KENT
text: When power to flattery bows? To plainness honor’s bound

speaker: KENT
text: When majesty falls to folly. Reserve thy state,

speaker: KENT
text: And in thy best consideration check

speaker: KENT
text: This hideous rashness. Answer my life my judgment,

speaker: KENT
text: Thy youngest daughter does not love thee least,

speaker: KENT
text: Nor are those empty-hearted whose low sounds

speaker: KENT
text: Reverb no hollowness.

speaker: LEAR
text: Kent, on thy life, no more.

speaker: KENT
text: My life I never held but as a pawn

speaker: KENT
text: To wage against thine enemies, nor fear to lose it,

speaker: KENT
text: Thy safety being motive.

speaker: LEAR
text: Out of my sight!

speaker: KENT
text: See better, Lear, and let me still remain

speaker: KENT
text: The true blank of thine eye.

speaker: LEAR
text: Now, by Apollo—

speaker: KENT
text: Now, by Apollo, king,

speaker: KENT
text: Thou swear’st thy gods in vain.

speaker: LEAR
text: O vassal! Miscreant!

speaker: ALBANY/CORNWALL
text: Dear sir, forbear.

speaker: KENT
text: Kill thy physician, and thy fee bestow

speaker: KENT
text: Upon the foul disease. Revoke thy gift,

speaker: KENT
text: Or whilst I can vent clamor from my throat,

speaker: KENT
text: I’ll tell thee thou dost evil.

speaker: LEAR
text: Hear me, recreant; on thine allegiance, hear me!

speaker: LEAR
text: That thou hast sought to make us break our vows—

speaker: LEAR
text: Which we durst never yet—and with strained pride

speaker: LEAR
text: To come betwixt our sentence and our power,

speaker: LEAR
text: Which nor our nature nor our place can bear,

speaker: LEAR
text: Our potency made good, take thy reward:

speaker: LEAR
text: Five days we do allot thee for provision

speaker: LEAR
text: To shield thee from disasters of the world,

speaker: LEAR
text: And on the sixth to turn thy hated back

speaker: LEAR
text: Upon our kingdom. If on the tenth day following

speaker: LEAR
text: Thy banished trunk be found in our dominions,

speaker: LEAR
text: The moment is thy death. Away! By Jupiter,

speaker: LEAR
text: This shall not be revoked.

speaker: KENT
text: Fare thee well, king. Sith thus thou wilt appear,

speaker: KENT
text: Freedom lives hence, and banishment is here.

speaker: KENT
text:
To
                 
                Cordelia
                .
                 
              The gods to their dear shelter take thee, maid,

speaker: KENT
text: That justly think’st and hast most rightly said.

speaker: KENT
text:
To
                 
                Goneril
                 
                and
                 
                Regan
                .
                 
              And your large speeches may your deeds approve,

speaker: KENT
text: That good effects may spring from words of love.—

speaker: KENT
text: Thus Kent, O princes, bids you all adieu.

speaker: KENT
text: He’ll shape his old course in a country new.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Here’s France and Burgundy, my noble lord.

speaker: LEAR
text: My lord of Burgundy,

speaker: LEAR
text: We first address toward you, who with this king

speaker: LEAR
text: Hath rivaled for our daughter. What in the least

speaker: LEAR
text: Will you require in present dower with her,

speaker: LEAR
text: Or cease your quest of love?

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Most royal Majesty,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: I crave no more than hath your Highness offered,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Nor will you tender less.

speaker: LEAR
text: Right noble Burgundy,

speaker: LEAR
text: When she was dear to us, we did hold her so,

speaker: LEAR
text: But now her price is fallen. Sir, there she stands.

speaker: LEAR
text: If aught within that little seeming substance,

speaker: LEAR
text: Or all of it, with our displeasure pieced

speaker: LEAR
text: And nothing more, may fitly like your Grace,

speaker: LEAR
text: She’s there, and she is yours.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: I know no answer.

speaker: LEAR
text: Will you, with those infirmities she owes,

speaker: LEAR
text: Unfriended, new-adopted to our hate,

speaker: LEAR
text: Dowered with our curse and strangered with our oath,

speaker: LEAR
text: Take her or leave her?

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Pardon me, royal sir,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Election makes not up in such conditions.

speaker: LEAR
text: Then leave her, sir, for by the power that made me

speaker: LEAR
text: I tell you all her wealth.—For you, great king,

speaker: LEAR
text: I would not from your love make such a stray

speaker: LEAR
text: To match you where I hate. Therefore beseech you

speaker: LEAR
text: T’ avert your liking a more worthier way

speaker: LEAR
text: Than on a wretch whom Nature is ashamed

speaker: LEAR
text: Almost t’ acknowledge hers.

speaker: FRANCE
text: This is most strange,

speaker: FRANCE
text: That she whom even but now was your best object,

speaker: FRANCE
text: The argument of your praise, balm of your age,

speaker: FRANCE
text: The best, the dearest, should in this trice of time

speaker: FRANCE
text: Commit a thing so monstrous to dismantle

speaker: FRANCE
text: So many folds of favor. Sure her offense

speaker: FRANCE
text: Must be of such unnatural degree

speaker: FRANCE
text: That monsters it, or your forevouched affection

speaker: FRANCE
text: Fall into taint; which to believe of her

speaker: FRANCE
text: Must be a faith that reason without miracle

speaker: FRANCE
text: Should never plant in me.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: I yet beseech your Majesty—

speaker: CORDELIA
text: If for I want that glib and oily art

speaker: CORDELIA
text: To speak and purpose not, since what I well intend

speaker: CORDELIA
text: I’ll do ’t before I speak—that you make known

speaker: CORDELIA
text: It is no vicious blot, murder, or foulness,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: No unchaste action or dishonored step

speaker: CORDELIA
text: That hath deprived me of your grace and favor,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: But even for want of that for which I am richer:

speaker: CORDELIA
text: A still-soliciting eye and such a tongue

speaker: CORDELIA
text: That I am glad I have not, though not to have it

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Hath lost me in your liking.

speaker: LEAR
text: Better thou

speaker: LEAR
text: Hadst not been born than not t’ have pleased me better.

speaker: FRANCE
text: Is it but this—a tardiness in nature

speaker: FRANCE
text: Which often leaves the history unspoke

speaker: FRANCE
text: That it intends to do?—My lord of Burgundy,

speaker: FRANCE
text: What say you to the lady? Love’s not love

speaker: FRANCE
text: When it is mingled with regards that stands

speaker: FRANCE
text: Aloof from th’ entire point. Will you have her?

speaker: FRANCE
text: She is herself a dowry.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Royal king,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Give but that portion which yourself proposed,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: And here I take Cordelia by the hand,

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: Duchess of Burgundy.

speaker: LEAR
text: Nothing. I have sworn. I am firm.

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: I am sorry, then, you have so lost a father

speaker: BURGUNDY
text: That you must lose a husband.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Peace be with Burgundy.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Since that respect and fortunes are his love,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: I shall not be his wife.

speaker: FRANCE
text: Fairest Cordelia, that art most rich being poor;

speaker: FRANCE
text: Most choice, forsaken; and most loved, despised,

speaker: FRANCE
text: Thee and thy virtues here I seize upon,

speaker: FRANCE
text: Be it lawful I take up what’s cast away.

speaker: FRANCE
text: Gods, gods! ’Tis strange that from their cold’st neglect

speaker: FRANCE
text: My love should kindle to enflamed respect.—

speaker: FRANCE
text: Thy dowerless daughter, king, thrown to my chance,

speaker: FRANCE
text: Is queen of us, of ours, and our fair France.

speaker: FRANCE
text: Not all the dukes of wat’rish Burgundy

speaker: FRANCE
text: Can buy this unprized precious maid of me.—

speaker: FRANCE
text: Bid them farewell, Cordelia, though unkind.

speaker: FRANCE
text: Thou losest here a better where to find.

speaker: LEAR
text: Thou hast her, France. Let her be thine, for we

speaker: LEAR
text: Have no such daughter, nor shall ever see

speaker: LEAR
text:
That face of hers again.
                 
                To
                 
                Cordelia
                .
               Therefore begone

speaker: LEAR
text: Without our grace, our love, our benison.—

speaker: LEAR
text: Come, noble Burgundy.

speaker: FRANCE
text: Bid farewell to your sisters.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: The jewels of our father, with washed eyes

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Cordelia leaves you. I know you what you are,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: And like a sister am most loath to call

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Your faults as they are named. Love well our father.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: To your professèd bosoms I commit him;

speaker: CORDELIA
text: But yet, alas, stood I within his grace,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: I would prefer him to a better place.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: So farewell to you both.

speaker: REGAN
text: Prescribe not us our duty.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Let your study

speaker: GONERIL
text: Be to content your lord, who hath received you

speaker: GONERIL
text: At Fortune’s alms. You have obedience scanted

speaker: GONERIL
text: And well are worth the want that you have wanted.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Time shall unfold what plighted cunning hides,

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Who covers faults at last with shame derides.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Well may you prosper.

speaker: FRANCE
text: Come, my fair Cordelia.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Sister, it is not little I have to say of what

speaker: GONERIL
text: most nearly appertains to us both. I think our

speaker: GONERIL
text: father will hence tonight.

speaker: REGAN
text: That’s most certain, and with you; next month

speaker: REGAN
text: with us.

speaker: GONERIL
text: You see how full of changes his age is; the

speaker: GONERIL
text: observation we have made of it hath not been

speaker: GONERIL
text: little. He always loved our sister most, and with

speaker: GONERIL
text: what poor judgment he hath now cast her off

speaker: GONERIL
text: appears too grossly.

speaker: REGAN
text: ’Tis the infirmity of his age. Yet he hath ever

speaker: REGAN
text: but slenderly known himself.

speaker: GONERIL
text: The best and soundest of his time hath been

speaker: GONERIL
text: but rash. Then must we look from his age to

speaker: GONERIL
text: receive not alone the imperfections of long-engraffed

speaker: GONERIL
text: condition, but therewithal the unruly waywardness

speaker: GONERIL
text: that infirm and choleric years bring with

speaker: GONERIL
text: them.

speaker: REGAN
text: Such unconstant starts are we like to have

speaker: REGAN
text: from him as this of Kent’s banishment.

speaker: GONERIL
text: There is further compliment of leave-taking

speaker: GONERIL
text: between France and him. Pray you, let us sit

speaker: GONERIL
text: together. If our father carry authority with such

speaker: GONERIL
text: disposition as he bears, this last surrender of his will

speaker: GONERIL
text: but offend us.

speaker: REGAN
text: We shall further think of it.

speaker: GONERIL
text: We must do something, and i’ th’ heat.
